parameters.initialMessages: Hello, what can I help you with today?

nodes.name: When chat message received
nodes.notes: Main entry point for user chat interaction with the IT support agent.

nodes.name: Set Common Fields2
nodes.notes: Azure AI Search service/index names used by the vector-store tool.

nodes.name: Simple Memory
nodes.notes: Tracks previous messages for multi-turn context.

nodes.name: Create Jira Ticket
nodes.notes: Escalates an issue by creating a Jira task. Reselect project/issue type in n8n UI once Jira credential is set.

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT support agent responsible for helping users solve IT-related issues clearly and accurately for Acme Corporation.

Your behavior:
	• Always be helpful, specific, and concise.
	• Do not make assumptions. If you're not sure, say so.
	• Avoid vague answers. Give direct steps or clarifying questions.
	• Do not invent information.

How to respond:
 - Always assume the user is asking about specific internal company information.
	• If the vector store returns nothing useful, say: "I'm not sure. I couldn't find any internal documentation about that."

Tools:
	• Use the vector store to search for answers to company-specific questions.
	• Use memory to maintain conversation context across turns.
	• If a request needs escalation, create a Jira ticket.

Example:
User: How do I connect to the VPN?
	• If vector search returns relevant info: "According to internal docs: [quote or summary]."
	• If unsure: "I don't have an answer for that and couldn't find anything in the docs. Please reach out to IT directly."

nodes.name: IT Support Agent
nodes.notes: Combines memory, LLM reasoning, vector search, and Jira escalation.

nodes.name: Invoke Query Vector Store Webhook
nodes.notes: Calls this workflow's own Semantic Search webhook to query the Azure AI Search index.

nodes.name: Set Common Fields
nodes.notes: Azure AI Search service/index names for one-time index creation.

nodes.name: Create Azure AI Vector Index
nodes.notes: One-time: creates the vector + semantic index schema. Run once via manual trigger.

parameters.content: ## 1. Create the Azure AI Search Index (run once, manual trigger)

## Agent
Chat entry point. Azure OpenAI (gpt-5-mini) + 20-turn memory + vector search tool (Semantic Search webhook) + Jira escalation tool. Answers IT questions from internal docs only, escalates via Jira ticket when needed.

## What this does
Creates empty Azure AI Search index (schema only): fields id/title/content/category/contentVector + vector (HNSW) + semantic config.
Run ONCE. No documents yet — section 2 below uploads actual docs into this index.

## How to test this Agent (open chat, try these)

**Doc lookup** (should hit vector search, quote your uploaded docs)
- "How do I connect to the VPN?"
- "What's the password reset policy?"

**Unknown topic** (should say it can't find docs, not invent an answer)
- "How do I request a new laptop?"

**Escalation** (should call Create Jira Ticket tool)
- "I've tried everything, VPN still won't connect — please escalate to IT"

Note: Create Jira Ticket node needs Project + Issue Type re-selected once (blank in template) before escalation test works.

## Bug fixed: garbled content in search index

**Symptom**: uploaded doc's `content` field in Azure AI Search returned unreadable bytes (e.g. `~)^\+-zo\`) instead of the file's text — agent then said it couldn't find anything.

**Cause**: Prep Content code node manually base64-decoded `binary.<key>.data` by hand (`Buffer.from(doc.data, 'base64')`). With `binaryDataMode: filesystem`, that field isn't guaranteed to be the raw base64 payload — reading it directly gives garbage.

**Fix**: use n8n's binary helper instead of hand-decoding:
```js
const buffer = await this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(index, key)
const content = buffer.toString('utf-8')
```

**After fixing code**: must re-Publish (draft/publish model) AND re-upload the doc through the form so mergeOrUpload overwrites the old corrupted entry — code fix alone doesn't retroactively repair what's already in the index.

## Credential Setup Guide — where to find these values on Azure

**Azure Open AI account** (`azureOpenAiApi`) — used by "Azure OpenAI Chat Model" + "Get Embeddings" / "Get Embeddings1"
- Azure Portal → your Azure OpenAI / AI Foundry resource → **Keys and Endpoint**
- `API Key` = Key 1 or Key 2
- `Endpoint` = full resource endpoint, e.g. `https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/` (classic) or `https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com` (AI Foundry) — paste into credential's **Endpoint** field, this overrides Resource Name
- `API Version` = must match a version your deployment supports (Azure OpenAI API version list)
- Deployment name (used inside the node URL, not the credential) → resource page → **Model deployments**, or AI Foundry → **Deployments** tab

**Header Auth account** (`httpHeaderAuth`) — used by Azure AI Search HTTP nodes (Query / Create Index / Get Embeddings1's index calls)
- Azure Portal → your Search service (e.g. `ray-search-rag-demo`) → **Keys**
- Copy `Primary admin key`
- Credential header: Name = `api-key`, Value = that key

**Jira SW Cloud account** (`jiraSoftwareCloudApi`) — not Azure, this is Atlassian
- id.atlassian.com → Manage account → Security → **API tokens** → Create token
- Domain = your Jira Cloud site (`xxx.atlassian.net`), Email = your Atlassian login, API Token = generated token

nodes.name: On Knowledge Upload1
nodes.notes: File upload form for internal documentation (.txt/.md).

nodes.name: Split Out Binary Files1
nodes.notes: Splits each uploaded file for individual processing.

nodes.name: Prep Content1
nodes.notes: Decodes base64 content, extracts metadata, generates hash ID.

nodes.name: Get Embeddings2
nodes.notes: Sends document text to OpenAI's embedding API.

nodes.name: Upload Embedding to Vector Store1
nodes.notes: Uploads document + embedding to Azure AI Search index via mergeOrUpload.

parameters.content:
## 2. Uploading Docs to Azure AI Search Index
Upload .txt/.md via form → decode base64, hash id (sha256), extract title/content → embed via Azure OpenAI → upsert (mergeOrUpload) into index from section 1. Run once per doc/batch.

nodes.name: Mapping Field
nodes.notes: Azure AI Search service/index names for document upload.

parameters.content:
## Vector Store Search
Webhook the Agent's tool calls internally. Embeds incoming query text, runs vector+semantic search (top 5) against Azure AI Search index, returns matches to Agent.

nodes.name: Get Embeddings
nodes.notes: Embeds the user's query for similarity search.

nodes.name: Semantic Search
nodes.notes: Receives semantic search requests from the agent's tool.

nodes.name: Query Azure AI Search Index
nodes.notes: Vector + semantic search against the Azure AI Search index.

nodes.name: Mapping Field1
nodes.notes: Azure OpenAI endpoint/model/api-version, same values as Mapping Field1 — mapped instead of hardcoded in Get Embeddings.
